title: plantago arborescens var. maderensis
intro: Plantago arborescens var. maderensis, also known as Madeiran plantain, is a variety of the plantain plant that is native to the island of Madeira. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of the Plantago arborescens var. maderensis have been traditionally used to support lung health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, offering relief from congestion and inflammation. Herbal preparations of Plantago arborescens var. maderensis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
